title: cheiranthus asperrimus
intro: Cheiranthus asperrimus, also known as the Rough Rockcress, is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant is also believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever and relieving coughs. Herbal preparations of Cheiranthus asperrimus typically involve drying the leaves and flowers, which can then be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
